CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2023 Set For Trial On Feb-26-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SHANNON SEIBERT, Esq. of Seibert Bautista Montoya (Tel: 510-679-1981) for plaintiff;
Atty. ALEXA R. HANLON, Esq. of Fisher & Phillips LLP (Tel: 415-490-9000) for defendants Scott Street Senior Housing Complex, Inc. dba Rhoda Goldman Plaza and Ira Kurtz.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SCOTT STREET SENIOR HOUSING COMPLEX, INC AND IRA KURTZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

"Defendants' demurrer to plaintiff's complaint" is overruled.

1 (discrimination [Gov. Code 12940(a)]): The complaint adequately pleads discrimination based on age, gender and disability. (Id. at 9:1-24; CACI 2500.) Defendants cite no authority for their notion that plaintiff must elect one of her three bases as "the substantial motivating reason" for discrimination. (Memo. 9:10-22.)

2 (harassment [Gov. Code 12940(j)]): The complaint adequately pleads harassment based on age, gender and disability. (Id. at 10:1-11:2; CACI 2521A.) Defendants cite no authority for their notions that plaintiff must elect one of her three bases as "the substantial motivating reason," "show" harassment at the pleading stage and plead it with specificity. (Memo. 9:25-10:9.)

3 (failure to prevent discrimination or harassment [Gov. Code 12940(k)]): The complaint adequately pleads this cause. (Id. at 11:4-12:2; CACI 2527.) Defendants again rely on their unsupported notion that plaintiff must elect one of her three discrimination bases. (Memo. 10:16-25.)

4 (retaliation [opposing discrimination or harassment: Gov. Code 12940(h)]): The complaint adequately pleads this cause. (Id. at 12:4-27; CACI 2505.) Defendants complain that retaliation for opposing discrimination is not pled (Memo. 11:7-8), but retaliation for opposing harassment is, and either suffices.

5 (retaliation [requesting an accommodation: Gov. Code 12940(m)(2)]): The complaint adequately pleads this cause, including adverse employment actions. (Id. at 13:1-23; CACI 2505; Memo. 11:22-23.)

6 (retaliation [Labor Code 1102.5(b), (c)]): The complaint adequately pleads this cause. (Id. at 14:1-15:2; CACI 4603.) Defendants complain that specific laws are not identified, but cite no authority holding that they must be. (Memo. 12:13-15.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2022 Continued To Jan-18-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
